# UC-22.63.6 · ISA-TR84.00.09 §4 integrated cybersecurity programme: SIS-relevant security-incident detection feed-back into Cybersecurity Risk Assessment register and Process Hazard Analysis (PHA) refresh trigger

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Anomaly, Risk &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*When a cyber event touches a safety system, the safety team must be told right away, the cyber-risk record updated, and a decision made on whether to re-do the broader safety study. We watch every safety-touching cyber event and make sure all three of those happen on time.*

---

## Description

Reconciles every SIS-zone or SIS-conduit cyber-event against three feed-back SLAs of ISA-TR84.00.09 §4: (i) acknowledged by the on-call FSM-cyber team within 5 minutes (high/critical) or 30 minutes (medium), (ii) linked to a CRA finding within 8 hours (per UC-22.63.2 register), and (iii) a PHA-refresh trigger decision (refresh-required or not) recorded within 24 hours for high/critical events. The PHA-refresh decision-process is the integration limb that distinguishes a mature ISA-TR84.00.09 programme from a tick-box one — a SIS-relevant cyber-event that COULD invalidate a PHA assumption (e.g., introducing a new failure-mode) MUST be discussed by the PHA team within a defined window.

## Value

ISA-TR84.00.09 §4 is the operational glue between cybersecurity detection and the safety-lifecycle. Without this glue, cyber-events accumulate in a SOC ticket-queue while the safety case becomes silently stale. The CSB and other post-incident bodies increasingly cite this 'silent staleness' as a contributory factor. The mature operating organisation runs this UC as the executive-level metric of how-fast-do-our-cyber-events-update-our-safety-case — typically reported to the Board Safety Committee monthly.

## Implementation

(1) Tag every Splunk OT Security event with `sis_relevance = true / false` via a `transforms.conf` lookup against `iec_61511_sis_zone_conduit_lookup`. (2) Maintain `iec_61511_sis_relevant_cyber_event_register_lookup` with the SLA timestamps captured as the FSM-cyber team works the event. (3) Schedule UC-22.63.6 hourly; route `risk_score = 100` to the on-call FSM-cyber team + Functional Safety Manager at severity P1 (paging).

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.63.6` archived hourly to `audit_evidence` with `iec_clause="ISA-TR84-00-09-s4"`. Every SIS-relevant cyber-event pins (event_id, sis_id, zone_id, conduit_id, event_type, mitre_ot_technique_id, severity, cra_finding_link_ref, pha_trigger_status, risk_score).

## Control test

### Positive scenario

An anomalous Modbus write to a SIS-zone PLC tag occurs at 14:32, but no CRA finding is opened and no PHA-refresh is logged. UC-22.63.6 MUST flag with risk_score=95.

### Negative scenario

The same event is detected, an alert opens within 5 minutes, a CRA finding (UC-22.63.2 register) is linked within the same shift, the Functional Safety Manager reviews and confirms the event does or does not trigger a PHA refresh, and the audit chain is closed within 24 hours. UC-22.63.6 MUST record risk_score=0.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- UC-22.63.2 (CRA register) and UC-22.63.5 (MoC register) deployed.
- An OT-detection capability (Claroty / Dragos / Nozomi / Tenable.ot) sending events to Splunk with SIS-zone tagging.
- A nominated FSM-cyber on-call rota.

## Step 1 - Zone-and-conduit lookup

```ini
[iec_61511_sis_zone_conduit_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.zone_id = string
field.conduit_id = string
field.sis_id = string
field.sl_target = string
field.sl_capability = string
field.sl_achieved = string
```

## Step 2 - SIS-relevant-cyber-event register

```ini
[iec_61511_sis_relevant_cyber_event_register_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.event_id = string
field.sis_id = string
field.event_type = string
field.severity = string
field.mitre_ot_technique_id = string
field.cra_finding_link_ref = string
field.pha_trigger_status = string
field.sla_acknowledged_at = time
field.sla_cra_linked_at = time
field.sla_pha_decided_at = time
field.dismissal_basis_ref = string
field.expected_write_pattern_ref = string
field.tool_maintenance_window_ref = string
```

## Step 3 - SIS-relevance enrichment

At index-time enrichment:

```ini
[ot_sis_relevance]
LOOKUP-sis-relevance = iec_61511_sis_zone_conduit_lookup zone_id, conduit_id OUTPUT sis_id AS sis_id, sl_target AS sl_target
EVAL-sis_relevance = if(isnotnull(sis_id), "true", "false")
```

## Step 4 - Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.63.6: Integrated SIS Cybersecurity Programme]
cron_schedule = 13 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -24h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.63.6,reg=IEC-61511,clause=ISA-TR84-00-09-s4
alert.severity = 5
```

## Step 5 - Functional-safety-specific notes

- **PHA-refresh decision criteria**: the FSM-cyber team should apply a documented decision-rubric (e.g., 'new failure-mode not in PHA' → PHA refresh; 'known failure-mode with previously-considered cyber-cause' → CRA update only).
- **Board reporting**: the SLA-conformance metric from this UC is typically the headline cyber-safety-bridge metric reported quarterly to the Board Safety Committee.

## SPL

```spl
index=ot_sis OR index=ot_security sis_relevance="true" earliest=-24h@h
| eval mitre_ot_technique_id = coalesce(mitre_ot_technique_id, mitre_attack_ics_technique)
| eval severity = coalesce(severity, alert_severity)
| stats values(mitre_ot_technique_id) as mitre_ot_technique_id, max(_time) as detected_at, latest(severity) as severity, latest(zone_id) as zone_id, latest(conduit_id) as conduit_id, latest(sis_id) as sis_id by event_id
| join type=left event_id [
    | inputlookup iec_61511_sis_relevant_cyber_event_register_lookup
    | table event_id cra_finding_link_ref pha_trigger_status sla_acknowledged_at sla_cra_linked_at sla_pha_decided_at
  ]
| eval now_ts = now()
| eval ack_lag_min = round((sla_acknowledged_at - detected_at) / 60, 1)
| eval cra_lag_hours = round((sla_cra_linked_at - detected_at) / 3600, 1)
| eval pha_lag_hours = round((sla_pha_decided_at - detected_at) / 3600, 1)
| eval no_ack = if(isnull(sla_acknowledged_at) AND (now_ts - detected_at) > 300, 1, 0)
| eval no_cra_link = if(isnull(sla_cra_linked_at) AND (now_ts - detected_at) > 28800, 1, 0)
| eval pha_undecided = if(isnull(sla_pha_decided_at) AND severity IN ("critical","high") AND (now_ts - detected_at) > 86400, 1, 0)
| eval risk_score = case(
    no_ack = 1, 100,
    no_cra_link = 1 AND severity IN ("critical","high"), 100,
    no_cra_link = 1, 95,
    pha_undecided = 1, 90,
    sla_pha_decided_at > sla_cra_linked_at + 86400, 70,
    ack_lag_min > 30 AND severity IN ("critical","high"), 60,
    1=1, 0)
| where risk_score > 0
| sort 0 -risk_score, +ack_lag_min
| table event_id sis_id zone_id conduit_id severity mitre_ot_technique_id detected_at ack_lag_min cra_lag_hours pha_lag_hours risk_score
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) Live SIS-relevant cyber-event timeline with SLA markers. (Panel 2) Acknowledgement / CRA-link / PHA-decision SLA conformance. (Panel 3) MITRE ATT&CK-ICS technique distribution. (Panel 4) Cyber-event → CRA finding → PHA-refresh funnel.

## Known False Positives

1. **Detection-tool maintenance window**: a planned maintenance window for an OT-detection tool may produce false detections — load `tool_maintenance_window_ref` to suppress. 2. **Known false-positive correlator**: certain Modbus / OPC-UA writes are operationally expected and well-understood — load `expected_write_pattern_ref` to suppress. 3. **Already-investigated and dismissed**: a cyber-event that has been investigated and dismissed with a documented determination (`dismissal_basis_ref`) is closed for SLA purposes.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `iec_61511_22_63_6_exceptions` with the Functional Safety Manager + CISO documented approval, an explicit event_id or pattern scope, an expiry date, and a written justification.

## References

- [ISA-TR84.00.09-2017 §4 — Integrated cybersecurity programme for the SIS](https://www.isa.org/standards-and-publications/isa-standards/isa-standards-committees/isa84)
- [ISA-TR84.00.09-2017 §5 — Cybersecurity risk assessment methodology](https://www.isa.org/standards-and-publications/isa-standards/isa-standards-committees/isa84)
- [CCPS Guidelines on Process Safety in Bioprocess Manufacturing (2023) — Integrated cyber/safety chapter](https://www.aiche.org/ccps)
